feedback.text: Hello I'm writing because there's several things that concern me in relation to digital landscape currently. First of all, the increasing collection of private citizens data from big tech/AI and the allowances that's being made for them to retain it as well as train on people's professional work. It's been proven that without the data, the generative AI models can't produce any high quality output. Yet the people who made that work is being stripped of their rights and protections, while the models are devaluation the original work they made as well as having the output compete with the original as it's flooding the markets. It's not only a devaluing of the product, it's a devaluation of human labour and the continuous noise will overwhelm communication channels. Even professional people who work in the business says so. See fx. cyber security export Nicholas Carlini (former Google Deepmind, now Anthropic) writing about the near harms https://nicholas.carlini.com/writing/2025/machines-of-ruthless-efficiency.html Furthermore, the only reason these giant companies are able to keep it going is, because of continuous investments from investors. They are losing money on their models, while depriving the people whose work their trained on of theirs. It undermines labour rights if people can simply take your work and run it through a model that can make near approximations based on your already often stolen data. On top of that big tech is utilising intense online scraping, driving up bills from website owners while taking their work without paying for it. This harms the open internet and sharing your work as a creator and consumer. The application of generative AI are also limited and often faulty. Just read up on all of the security breaches and leaks caused by chatbots leaking user data. It's not secure and no matter how many guardrails are put up as of writing, it won't be. There's always a way to bypass. I beckon the EU to invest in consumer friendly technology that works instead of experimental, costly near approximation models build with vibe coding that leaves major security flaws and will come to harm consumers in the future. When that's said. I'd also like to oppone against the increasingly terrible SaaS allowances and practires where companies can retroactively change their ToS to steal your data for training, add forced arbitration, retroactively remove access and subscription fees that didn't apply when you purchased physical as well as digital products. Please improve on right to own and right to repair principles for consumers and small businesses. I'd also like for the EU to consider the impact of most Europeans being reliant on payment processors and platforms from the US determining what we can and can't buy on those platforms as adults EU citizens. And I'd like for the EU to consider not furthering surveillance of their own citizens based on third-party apps as consumers are subjected to leaks of their personal info on the World Wide Web where it can be abused by bad actors. I myself have been subjected to people doing so with my personal email for car ride services in Brazil, Robinhood and various betting services etc. and these companies refuse to let me delete these accounts. Platforms such as Meta etc. preying on EU citizens rights, not respecting GDPR and lobbying for further control over our data is also not something the EU should allow. And please respect our privacy. I keep seeing leaked information from public and private data companies and it's the consumers affected who ends up having to deal with the fallout in their personal life. Thank you very much.
